confidence_rationale: Direct and adjacent competitors are identifiable, but the exact overlap depends on Source-Blendr's selected first vertical and output workflow.

direct_competitors.name: ASI ESP+
direct_competitors.category: Promotional products sourcing and distributor platform
direct_competitors.differentiation_gap_for_source_blendr: ESP+ appears strongest in promo sourcing/network workflows; Source-Blendr must differentiate through service aggregation, production recipes, margin modeling, and cross-vendor custom offer construction.

direct_competitors.name: Printavo
validated_capabilities: Screen printing, embroidery, sign, and digital print workflows
direct_competitors.differentiation_gap_for_source_blendr: Printavo validates shop operations but Source-Blendr can focus upstream on supplier/service aggregation, cost composition, bundles, and reusable resale offerings.

indirect_competitors.name: Spreadsheets plus supplier portals
indirect_competitors.reason: Current workflows may be flexible, familiar, and free enough to resist replacement.

indirect_competitors.name: Generic CRM, accounting, ecommerce, and document tools
indirect_competitors.reason: HubSpot, Salesforce, QuickBooks, Shopify, Google Drive, and proposal tools can cover fragments of the workflow without unified supplier modeling.

existing_alternatives: Manual PDF catalog and price book creation

saturation_risk.summary: The market has strong incumbents in promo sourcing and print-shop operations, but Source-Blendr's combined product-service-cost-bundle-resale model remains a plausible differentiation wedge.

differentiation_opportunities.reason: Many adjacent tools center either product sourcing or shop/order management; Source-Blendr can own combined supplier product and service cost modeling.

differentiation_opportunities.opportunity: Production recipes and reusable resale bundles
differentiation_opportunities.reason: Recipe-based cost and pricing logic directly supports custom offerings such as apparel plus decoration, awards plus engraving, or sign plus installation.

differentiation_opportunities.opportunity: Margin-first quote and price book output
differentiation_opportunities.reason: Source-Blendr can position around profitability controls rather than only sourcing, cataloging, or order management.

positioning_gaps.gap: The product category is broad and risks sounding like ERP, CRM, catalog software, quote software, and sourcing software at once.
positioning_gaps.mitigation: Use a narrow first-positioning statement around supplier products plus services into profitable resale quotes.

evidence.summary: ASI positions ESP+ as a single platform for promo business, product search, supplier relationships, catalogs, and websites.
evidence.confidence_effect: raises competitor confidence and incumbent threat level
evidence.limitations: Does not fully expose detailed pricing, workflow, or API capabilities.

evidence.summary: Printavo lists quotes, invoices, product catalogs, purchase orders, customer management, reporting, and automation features for print shops.
evidence.limitations: Does not validate Source-Blendr's cross-supplier aggregation feature.

recommendations: Do not position against every incumbent at once.
Use a wedge such as: profitable product-plus-service resale quoting from supplier data.
Carry competitive overlap into Stage 3 as modular capability boundaries rather than monolithic ERP scope.
